issue_trigger: An article about Chinese characters uses emotional hooks like 'you won't believe this' and 'stigmatization' to get clicks, and some of its claims turn out to be wrong.
court_specific_goal: Provide a practical, ground-level take on clickbait ethics: does it matter if viral content is inaccurate if it makes people think about gender issues? Express whether the emotional framing feels manipulative or just normal internet behavior from an everyday consumer perspective.
